question: Explain the purpose of a training set and a test set in the context of machine learning. Why is it important to keep these datasets separate?
expectedPoints: The training set is used to teach (train) the model by adjusting its internal parameters.
The test set is a held-out dataset used exclusively to evaluate the final model's performance on unseen data.
Keeping them separate prevents data leakage and provides an unbiased estimate of the model's generalization ability, ensuring the model is evaluated on data it has not learned from.

question: Describe the k-fold cross-validation process. What is its primary advantage over a simple train/test split?
expectedPoints: k-fold CV involves randomly partitioning the dataset into k equally sized subsets (folds).
The process iterates k times, each time using a different fold as the test set and the remaining k-1 folds as the training set.
Performance metrics from each iteration are averaged to produce a more robust estimate of model performance.

Its primary advantage is that it uses all data for both training and testing, reducing the variance of the performance estimate compared to a single train/test split, which can be highly dependent on how the data was partitioned.

question: Compare and contrast the issues of overfitting and underfitting. How can the learning curves of a model (plots of error vs. dataset size) be used to diagnose and address each problem?
expectedPoints: Overfitting occurs when a model learns the training data too well, including its noise, resulting in high variance and poor performance on new data. Underfitting occurs when a model is too simple to capture the underlying pattern of the data, resulting in high bias and poor performance on both training and test sets.
A learning curve for an overfit model will show a large gap between a very low training error and a high validation error. As more data is added, the validation error may slowly decrease.
A learning curve for an underfit model will show high and converging training and validation errors, indicating that the model is not learning effectively.

To address overfitting: use techniques like regularization, pruning, or getting more training data. The learning curve can show if more data will help by seeing if the validation error continues to decrease.

To address underfitting: use a more complex model, add new features, or reduce regularization. The learning curve will show if the model has high bias, as the errors will remain high even with large amounts of data.
